entries.value: Target course completion rate is >80%. Industry average for online courses is 15-25%, so >50% is already strong. Calculate as completed_enrollments / total_enrollments * 100. Track by course and cohort to identify problem courses.

Target student satisfaction is >4.2 out of 5.0. Measure via post-course surveys and ongoing ratings. Courses below 3.5 should trigger content review. Satisfaction correlates strongly with completion and re-enrollment.

Target average assessment score is >75%. Scores below 60% across a course may indicate poor instruction or overly difficult assessments. Scores above 95% may indicate assessments are too easy and not driving learning.
Target enrollment growth is >10% quarter-over-quarter. Track both new student enrollments and returning student re-enrollments separately. Organic growth (non-paid) above 5% QoQ is healthy.

Target instructor rating is >4.0 out of 5.0. Instructors below 3.5 should receive coaching or content improvement support. Top instructors (>4.5) should be featured and given priority for new course creation.

Target re-enrollment rate is >40% of completing students enrolling in another course within 90 days. Strong indicator of platform value. Track by category — students who complete a series re-enroll at higher rates.

Trigger an alert when an active student has no course activity for 14+ days. Early intervention (email nudge, progress reminder) within the first 21 days of inactivity recovers 30-40% of at-risk students.
Optimal online course structure: modules of 15-20 minutes, assessment every 3-4 modules, project-based capstone. Courses over 40 hours should be split into a learning path of 3-5 shorter courses.

A learning path is an ordered sequence of courses that builds expertise in a domain. Paths typically contain 3-7 courses progressing from beginner to advanced. Completing a path earns a specialization certificate.

Mix assessment types: 60% quizzes (knowledge retention), 25% projects (application), 15% peer reviews (collaboration). Allow 2-3 attempts on quizzes to encourage learning from mistakes. Final exams should be single-attempt.
